D11 ComputationalL2 UnderstandReference

AVIF vs JPEG — 30-Year Improvement

AVIF (AV1 stills, 2019) typically delivers JPEG-equivalent quality at 1/3 to 1/2 the bytes thanks to better prediction, transforms, and entropy coding

JPEG (1992) uses DCT + zig-zag + Huffman. AVIF (2019) uses HEVC-class block prediction, multiple transform sizes (4×4 to 64×64), arithmetic coding (CABAC), and supports 10-bit + wide gamut. Catch: AVIF encoding is ~50× slower than JPEG, decode is ~5× slower.

Slide quality level to see file-size approximation curves.
